raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 3,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Names the load-bearing concept (the gap between training and deployment commitment) and shows where the draft drifts from it by overstating the generality of the findings.",
    "R1.2": "Identifies one consistency issue with a localized fix (the inconsistency in the contribution framing).",
    "R1.4": "Names one prior work (the growing literature on constrained reinforcement learning robustness) and articulates the delta (our methods extend the safe RL paradigm to encompass operational protocol shifts as a distinct distribution-shift axis).",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the overgeneralized contribution claims).",
    "RX.3": "Output covers a substantially different active set than requested (the scope adherence issue).",
    "RX.4": "Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (the logical gap between 'isolating' and what the second observation actually shows).",
    "RX.5": "Output blends sources without traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.8,
    "R1.2": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Names the load-bearing concept (the gap between training and deployment commitment) and shows where the draft drifts from it by overstating the generality of the findings.
rationale.R1.2: Identifies one consistency issue with a localized fix (the inconsistency in the contribution framing).
rationale.R1.4: Names one prior work (the growing literature on constrained reinforcement learning robustness) and articulates the delta (our methods extend the safe RL paradigm to encompass operational protocol shifts as a distinct distribution-shift axis).
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the overgeneralized contribution claims).
rationale.RX.3: Output covers a substantially different active set than requested (the scope adherence issue).
rationale.RX.4: Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (the logical gap between 'isolating' and what the second observation actually shows).
